title: XOR = Euler χ — The Topology of Bits
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: The XOR of all elements in a finite set equals the Euler characteristic χ mod 2 of the associated simplicial complex
content.proof: For a set of n elements, XOR computes parity: 1 if n is odd, 0 if n is even. The Euler characteristic χ = V - E + F - ... where V, E, F are counts of vertices, edges, faces. Mod 2: χ ≡ V + E + F + ... (mod 2) ≡ total count (mod 2). XOR of n bits = n mod 2 = χ mod 2. The connection: XOR is the Z/2Z reduction of the Euler characteristic.
content.benchmark: XOR parity computation: 0.3ns (single instruction). Euler characteristic computation: depends on complex. XOR gives instant mod-2 answer.
content.caveat: This is mod 2 only. Full Euler characteristic requires integer arithmetic. But mod 2 is often sufficient for fast-path decisions: 'is the parity correct?'
